name: 9/11 Mysteries Documentary
description: The '9/11 Mysteries' documentary, released in 2006, is a prominent example of 9/11 conspiracy theories. It presents arguments suggesting that the collapse of the World Trade Center towers and 7 World Trade Center were not solely caused by the impact of the airplanes and subsequent fires. The documentary posits that controlled demolition using explosives was the primary cause of the collapses. This theory originated and gained traction in the years following the attacks, fueled by skepticism of the official 9/11 Commission Report and perceived inconsistencies in the available evidence. Proponents point to the speed and symmetry of the building collapses, the presence of molten metal in the debris, and witness testimonies suggesting explosions as evidence of controlled demolition. Mainstream scientific and engineering consensus, as represented by the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) reports, attributes the collapses to structural damage caused by the plane impacts and subsequent fires weakening the steel support structures. Critics argue that the 'molten metal' was likely aluminum from the planes mixing with other materials, the speed of collapse is consistent with fire-induced structural failure, and witness testimonies are unreliable and often misinterpreted. The documentary remains a significant touchstone for 9/11 conspiracy theories, promoting the idea of government complicity or negligence in the attacks.
subtitle: Explosives, cover-ups, and the truth of 9/11?

claims.claim: The World Trade Center towers and 7 World Trade Center were brought down by controlled demolition.
claims.evidence: Symmetry of the collapses, presence of molten metal, witness accounts of explosions, rapid speed of collapse.
claims.criticism: NIST reports attribute collapses to fire-induced structural failure; 'molten metal' is likely aluminum residue; witness accounts are unreliable and often misinterpreted; speed of collapse is consistent with progressive collapse.

claims.claim: The official 9/11 Commission Report is a cover-up.
claims.evidence: Perceived inconsistencies in the report, lack of thorough investigation into alternative explanations, suppression of dissenting voices.
claims.criticism: The 9/11 Commission Report is a comprehensive investigation based on extensive evidence and testimony; dissenting voices were considered but ultimately deemed unpersuasive based on scientific and factual analysis.

claims.claim: Elements within the US government had prior knowledge of the attacks and allowed them to happen, or actively participated in them.
claims.evidence: Alleged insider trading before the attacks, failure of NORAD to intercept the planes, suspicious activities of government officials.
claims.criticism: Investigations found no evidence of insider trading; NORAD response was delayed due to unprecedented nature of the attacks; suspicious activities are coincidental or misinterpreted.

keyFigures.name: Dylan Avery
keyFigures.role: Director of 'Loose Change' documentary, a key influence.

keyFigures.name: David Ray Griffin
keyFigures.role: Author of numerous books promoting 9/11 conspiracy theories.

keyFigures.name: Steven Jones
keyFigures.role: Physicist who claimed to find nano-thermite in WTC dust.

keyFigures.name: David Chandler
keyFigures.role: Physicist who argued for controlled demolition based on video analysis.

keyFigures.name: Thomas Eagar
keyFigures.role: MIT Professor of Materials Engineering, critic of controlled demolition theories.

keyFigures.name: Zdeněk P. Bažant
keyFigures.role: Professor of Civil Engineering and Materials Science at Northwestern University, developed a model of progressive collapse.

mythologyConnections: The idea of a grand conspiracy controlling world events echoes themes of hidden power structures found in various mythologies.
The search for truth and uncovering hidden secrets is a common motif in heroic myths.

timeline.event: Early conspiracy theories about 9/11 begin to emerge online.
Continued debate and proliferation of 9/11 conspiracy theories.

sources.author: David Ray Griffin
